name: LLMService_call_llm_handles_alternative_response_shapes_under_load
description: Performance/security hybrid test ensuring LLMService.call_llm correctly extracts content and tokens_used from responses with atypical shapes under repeated fast calls (stress). Verifies no sensitive fields are leaked in returned LLMResponse.raw_payload when response lacks __dict__ or usage shapes vary.
input: Instantiate LLMService with openai_client_factory returning a stub client whose chat.completions.create returns several variant response shapes quickly across repeated calls: (1) object with choices[0].message.content and usage attr, (2) object with choices list but choices[0].message missing content attribute, (3) object with no usage. Run 100 short sequential calls to call_llm and record results and durations.
output: All calls return LLMResponse objects without raising exceptions; the method extracts empty string when content missing; tokens_used is present only when usage available; aggregate latency per call remains low (e.g., avg < 150ms).
success_criteria: no exceptions propagated from call_llm in any variant
when response has content -> response.text equals that content
tokens_used is None when response.usage missing
average call latency across 100 calls < 150 ms
error_scenarios: stubbed client raises intermittently producing exceptions
unexpected response shapes that break indexing into response.choices leading to IndexError
test harness too slow causing latency assertions to fail
